questions.question: PT of a weld shows a small star of radial linear indications exactly where the arc was broken abruptly. What is it, and why does it matter despite its size?
options: Aligned porosity along the centerline, evaluated only as a grouped row
A hot tear — weld stops are where thick and thin sections meet
A crater crack — the unfilled crater solidified and shrank with nothing feeding it, and crater cracks are favored starting points for larger cracks in service
Grinding checks from post-weld dressing, which are cosmetic only
questions.explanation: An abruptly broken arc leaves an unfilled crater that solidifies and shrinks with no feed metal, cracking into a radial star or a single short line. Small as they are, crater cracks are preferred initiation sites for service cracks. Aligned porosity reads as a row of rounded dots, and hot tears belong to casting section junctions.

questions.question: Why do specifications schedule penetrant testing of hardenable steel parts AFTER heat treatment rather than before?
options: Quench cracks form at or soon after heat treat — letting go at keyways, holes, and sharp corners — so an inspection performed before quenching cannot find them
Hardened surfaces hold penetrant by capillary action better than soft ones
Heat treatment opens pre-existing laps and seams so they bleed more strongly
Penetrant materials are qualified only for use on hardened steel
questions.explanation: Quenching combines violent thermal gradients with the martensite volume change, and the stresses let go at geometric stress raisers — keyways, holes, threads, sharp corners. The cracks appear at or soon after heat treat, so PT is sequenced after it; testing earlier would stamp parts clean that crack in the quench.

questions.question: Which three conditions must exist at the same time for stress-corrosion cracking, and what indication pattern does it produce?
options: Cyclic stress, elevated temperature, and a hardened microstructure — a single sharp line
Compressive residual stress, chlorides, and plain carbon steel — scattered rounded dots
Hydrogen charging, electroplating, and high strength — delayed fine cracks at weld toes
Sustained tensile stress, a specific aggressive environment, and a susceptible alloy — branched, forked, spider-like colonies of fine cracks
questions.explanation: SCC needs all three at once: sustained tensile stress (applied or residual), a specific environment, and a susceptible alloy — austenitic stainless steel with chlorides is the textbook pair. The signature is branched, spider-like colonies, and corrosion product inside the cracks can choke penetrant entry, so even deep SCC may bleed weakly. The hydrogen option describes pickling and plating cracks, a processing problem.

questions.question: One forging shows a faint, wavy, intermittent linear indication at a fillet near the flash line. Another shows a straight, tight, continuous line running parallel to the grain direction. The most likely identities are
options: both are bursts, which always reach the as-forged surface
the wavy one is a lap — folded-over metal, oxide-lined, meeting the surface at a shallow angle; the straight one is a seam carried in on the bar stock
the wavy one is a seam; the straight one is a lap at the flash line
the wavy one is a cold shut; the straight one is a plate lamination on the rolled face
questions.explanation: Laps live at flash lines, fillets, and re-entrant corners: a fin of metal folds over and is pressed in without fusing, so the tight, oxide-lined, shallow-angle opening reads wavy, faint, and intermittent. Seams ride in on the bar stock and run straight and parallel to the grain. Bursts are internal and meet PT on machined faces, cold shuts are casting features, and laminations never show on rolled faces — only on cut edges.

questions.question: Why does a thorough PT of extruded bar pay special attention to the sawed ends, and which extrusion discontinuity runs TRANSVERSE on the outside surface?
options: Central bursts and back-end pipe hide inside the extrusion and surface only where a cut or machining exposes them; hot shortness (speed cracking) tears the skin in short transverse cracks
Laminations migrate to the bar ends during extrusion, and die lines run transverse to the flow
Crater cracks concentrate at cut ends, while laps run transverse across the skin
The ends cool fastest and quench-crack, while stringers run transverse to the working direction
questions.explanation: Billet pipe and oxidized billet skin flow toward the back end of the extrusion, and central bursts — chevron-shaped ruptures from too-severe reduction — ride the centerline; both are internal until a saw cut or machining exposes them, so cut ends get inspected. Running too hot or too fast tears the surface in short transverse cracks, the one extrusion discontinuity that runs across the flow; die lines are shallow and longitudinal, and stringers follow the working direction.

questions.question: A single round indication on a weld bleeds heavily and re-forms after each permitted wipe-and-redevelop. The best reading is
options: a shallow crater shrinkage pit — one weak bloom is typical
a lint fiber sitting on the surface
piping porosity — a small surface mouth over a large elongated gas channel, so the cavity below keeps feeding the bleed-out
a fatigue crack starting at the weld toe
questions.explanation: Heavy, regenerating bleed-out from a round mouth means real volume below the surface: piping porosity is an elongated gas channel reaching the surface through a pinhole. A shallow pit gives one weak bloom and quits, lint lifts away and never redevelops, and a fatigue crack would re-bleed as a continuous line, not a round dot.

questions.question: After honest reprocessing, an indication still cannot be positively established as false or nonrelevant. How does a Level II handle it?
options: Accept the part — unresolved doubt favors the hardware
Classify it nonrelevant if it lies anywhere near a design feature
Wipe it repeatedly until it stops returning, then accept
Treat it as relevant — the burden of proof never points the other way — resolving doubt only by reprocessing or one permitted wipe-and-redevelop verification
questions.explanation: Doubt resolves toward relevant. The honest tools are complete reprocessing and re-examination, or a single wipe-and-redevelop check — an indication that returns in the same place is telling the truth. Repeated wiping strips the flaw of the very penetrant you need, and nonrelevant is a conclusion defended with the part configuration, never a shrug.

questions.question: Which statement uses the vocabulary of PT evaluation correctly?
options: Every discontinuity found by penetrant testing is a defect and must be rejected
A discontinuity becomes a defect only when evaluation shows it exceeds the governing acceptance criteria
Defect and indication are interchangeable terms for any visible bleed-out
A nonrelevant indication is, by definition, a false indication
questions.explanation: A discontinuity is an interruption in the part's normal structure; it is a defect only after evaluation shows it exceeds the acceptance criteria — calling every bleed-out a defect is an evaluation you have not performed. Nonrelevant indications are TRUE entrapments caused by part configuration (press fits, keyways, threads), which is exactly what separates them from false indications like lint and wash residue.

questions.question: Which set of process-control limits matches ASTM E1417?
options: Part and penetrant at 32–150 °F; 5-minute dwell; rinse at 60 psi; dryer at 200 °F; 5 minutes of development

Part and penetrant between 40 and 125 °F; at least 10 minutes of wet penetrant dwell; rinse at 50–100 °F and 40 psi maximum; parts held at or below 160 °F in the dryer; at least 10 minutes of development
Part and penetrant between 40 and 125 °C; a 30-minute dwell during which the film may dry; rinse temperature uncontrolled
Any part temperature as long as dwell is doubled; rinse up to 100 psi; development read immediately
questions.explanation: E1417 holds part and penetrant between 40 and 125 °F (4–52 °C), requires a minimum 10-minute dwell with the film kept wet (a dried film stops feeding flaws and forces full reprocessing), limits the rinse to 50–100 °F at 40 psi maximum, caps part temperature in the dryer at 160 °F (71 °C), and requires at least 10 minutes of development before reading. The Celsius option smuggles the Fahrenheit band into the wrong scale.

questions.question: ASTM E1417 limits the water content of open-tank water-washable penetrant to 5 percent, checked monthly. Why is accumulating rinse water dangerous?
options: Water raises fluorescent brightness above the calibrated range
Water freezes inside flaws and mechanically widens them
Drip-back water slowly turns the penetrant into its own remover, so it washes out of the very flaws it should be highlighting
Water contamination only matters because it corrodes the tank walls
questions.explanation: Water-washable penetrant already contains its own emulsifying chemistry, so rinse water dripping back into the tank progressively converts the penetrant into its own remover — flaw entrapments wash away during rinsing and parts simply look clean. That silent failure mode is why the monthly check carries a hard 5 percent ceiling; brightness degradation is a separate quarterly E1135 comparison.
